jokes.text: Oh, hi, super group Boyz II Men, please let us win your contest. We sound, eh, pretty good.
jokes.setup: Mac says they sounded 'pretty good' at their rehearsal
jokes.punchline: Dennis sarcastically mocks the mediocrity with exaggerated politeness
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis perfectionist control freak behavior, delivered with his signature condescending sarcasm

jokes.text: Frank, we're not gonna die of the flu because we're young and vital. You are old and feeble.
jokes.setup: Frank worries about a deadly flu outbreak
jokes.punchline: Dennis dismisses concern by brutally categorizing Frank as expendable
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Dennis's narcissism and casual cruelty to Frank

jokes.text: 'Cause you know what you can't do when you get the flu? Sing? Sing. Sing.
jokes.setup: Dennis asks rhetorical question building to obvious answer
jokes.punchline: Multiple people jump in to answer, creating awkward overlap
jokes.explanation: Good awkward comedy as the group dynamics create an uncomfortable moment

jokes.text: Quarantine, baby! All right, this is gonna be good. Everybody find a spot to put your sleeping bags and we'll be all set.
jokes.setup: They're supposedly in a serious health quarantine
jokes.punchline: Mac treats it like a fun sleepover camp activity
jokes.explanation: Mac's childlike enthusiasm for what should be a serious situation is perfectly in character

jokes.text: You know, you don't have to sleep together. It's a comfort thing.
jokes.setup: Charlie and Frank have positioned their sleeping bags together
jokes.punchline: Frank defensively explains their unusual sleeping arrangement
jokes.explanation: Visual gag of their weird sleeping arrangement combined with Frank's awkward justification

jokes.text: Oh, my God, for the last time, we're not playing quarantine, this is a quarantine. Huh? I'm gonna get so blacked out.
jokes.setup: Dennis tries to establish the seriousness of their quarantine
jokes.punchline: Mac completely ignores this and focuses on getting drunk
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Dennis's seriousness and Mac's party mindset

jokes.text: Going house to house, handling money, banging lonely broads. They're filthy!
jokes.setup: Frank explains why pizza delivery guys are disease vectors
jokes.punchline: Escalates from reasonable concerns to 'banging lonely broads'
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank logic that starts reasonable and veers into crude absurdity

jokes.text: My mom used to vaccinate me, like, every month, and I never got sick.
jokes.setup: Discussion about vaccines working
jokes.punchline: Charlie reveals his mother gave him monthly vaccinations (which is insane)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie backstory detail that's both absurd and reveals his mother's overprotectiveness

jokes.text: The reason you didn't get sick was because you and your mom would wear those ridiculous bubble boy suits during the flu season.
jokes.setup: Charlie claims vaccines kept him healthy
jokes.punchline: Dennis reveals they wore literal bubble suits
jokes.explanation: Visual gag of imagining Charlie and his mom in bubble suits, perfectly escalating the absurdity

jokes.text: So I'll do, like, a stutter, and I'll say I got it from-from watching, like, a friend get blown to bits in Iraq.
jokes.setup: Dennis suggests they need a sob story for the singing competition
jokes.punchline: Casually suggests faking PTSD from watching friends die
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy showing Dennis's sociopathic willingness to fake trauma for personal gain

jokes.text: And do it with a Southern accent and talk about Jesus a lot.
jokes.setup: Dennis suggests faking war trauma
jokes.punchline: Mac suggests adding religious stereotypes to make it more manipulative
jokes.explanation: Mac's addition shows how they keep making their fake story more exploitative

jokes.text: Religious stuttering army carnies. Who love Jesus. And watched our friends explode on stage.
jokes.setup: They keep adding elements to their fake backstory
jokes.punchline: The story becomes completely ridiculous and contradictory
jokes.explanation: Good escalation comedy as their fake story becomes impossibly convoluted

jokes.text: Spider-Man. That's very clever, yeah, yeah, yeah.
jokes.setup: Someone ordered pizza under a fake name during quarantine
jokes.punchline: The fake name is 'Spider-Man' and Dennis sarcastically compliments it
jokes.explanation: Dennis's sarcastic response to an obviously juvenile fake name

jokes.text: Dennis, ask him how it's possible for him to talk to you through a cut phone wire. How is it possible for you to talk to me through a cut ph... I cut it when I found the pizza.
jokes.setup: Mac points out logical flaw in Dennis's investigation
jokes.punchline: Dennis starts to ask then realizes he cut the phone himself
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment where Dennis's paranoia undermines his own investigation

jokes.text: I can fight off whatever stupid illness this pizza may or may not have had, all right? I'm in perfect control of my body. Just like I am with my voice. I-I-I can go from flaccid to erect at a moment's notice.
jokes.setup: Dennis boasts about his physical control and perfect health
jokes.punchline: Pivots to demonstrating control over his penis
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis narcissism and sexual inappropriateness that makes everyone uncomfortable

jokes.text: He can, I've seen it. Buddy, you're seeing it. You're all seeing it. 'Cause I'm doing it right now. Flaccid, erect. Flaccid, erect. Flaccid, erect.
jokes.setup: Dennis claims control over his penis
jokes.punchline: Mac confirms it and Dennis demonstrates while narrating
jokes.explanation: Escalates the awkwardness perfectly with Mac's support and Dennis's live demonstration

jokes.text: Not too hard, not too soft.
jokes.setup: Dennis just demonstrated penis control
jokes.punchline: Quotes the Boyz II Men lyrics they were singing earlier in new context
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that recontextualizes innocent song lyrics in the most inappropriate way possible

jokes.text: Like, are they bat boys or are they butlers? You don't know, but it's like a sweet look.
jokes.setup: Mac describes their costume concept of bow ties and ball caps
jokes.punchline: Creates confusing hybrid identity that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Mac's confusion about their aesthetic creates a funny identity crisis for their look

jokes.text: Well, you said that we're all in the same outfit, right? So I'm trying to figure, like, who goes where. 'Same' as in 'matching,' not same as in jammed into the same set of clothing.
jokes.setup: Discussion about wearing the same outfit
jokes.punchline: Charlie interprets this as literally sharing one outfit between multiple people
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - takes simple instruction and interprets it in most bizarre way possible

jokes.text: D-D-D-Don't- Don't put me on the spot. I-I'm just, I-I-I'm just a Jesus-loving e-e-ex-marine.
jokes.setup: Dee is asked her opinion while practicing their fake stuttering personas
jokes.punchline: She stays in character with exaggerated stutter and fake backstory
jokes.explanation: Dee committing to the ridiculous fake persona they created earlier

jokes.text: This Bible's a Bible of my daddy who just di-di-died in my-my arms of-of throat cancer, from-from eating some-some-some bad pussy.
jokes.setup: Dee continues her fake tragic backstory with the Bible prop
jokes.punchline: Takes it from touching (father dying of cancer) to crude (bad pussy)
jokes.explanation: Classic Sunny escalation from fake sympathy-worthy tragedy to crude absurdity

jokes.text: No snow crabs! The shells could lacerate your throats. Absolutely not.
jokes.setup: Frank suggests eating snow crab during quarantine
jokes.punchline: Dennis has bizarrely specific concern about throat damage from shells
jokes.explanation: Dennis's obsession with protecting their voices leads to absurd food restrictions

jokes.text: He can do really cool stuff with his dick, though. You gotta ease up on talking about dicks, man. Like, it's just, it's too much by this point.
jokes.setup: Charlie casually mentions Dennis's dick abilities
jokes.punchline: Mac has to tell Charlie he talks about dicks too much
jokes.explanation: Meta moment where Mac has to address Charlie's recurring inappropriate fixation

jokes.text: Shit, I'm sorry, dude. I forgot, there's no opening in yours. But my mom rigged hers with a special pouch so that she could have her cocktails.
jokes.setup: Charlie tries to offer Mac a drink through his bubble suit
jokes.punchline: Reveals his mom modified her suit specifically for drinking alcohol
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character detail that his alcoholic mom engineered her quarantine suit for drinking

jokes.text: Your mom rigged her suit so she could drink booze? Dude, you mom's an alcoholic. My mom's not an alcoholic! No, bro! She is, but it's totally cool, because it skips a generation. That's why you're not an alcoholic.
jokes.setup: Mac points out Charlie's mom is clearly an alcoholic
jokes.punchline: Charlie defends her with completely made-up genetics about alcoholism
jokes.explanation: Charlie's complete denial and fake science to defend his obviously alcoholic mother

jokes.text: What is this? I love it! Oh, it's just a little dance move I've been working on. It's stupid. No, man, that's sweet as hell. Show me that shit.
jokes.setup: Mac accidentally does some kind of move
jokes.punchline: Charlie is genuinely impressed by Mac's 'dance move' and wants to see more
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment of Charlie genuinely supporting Mac's random physical comedy

jokes.text: You got a breach! Oh, shit! Come on. Hey! Close your pit! Close your pit! Don't tell Frank.
jokes.setup: Mac's dance move rips his bubble suit
jokes.punchline: Charlie panics about the 'breach' like it's a military emergency
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy escalating from dance to emergency breach protocol

jokes.text: Look at all the hair. Look at the hair. It's like a goddamn barber shop.
jokes.setup: Frank is paranoid about germs in the quarantine
jokes.punchline: Fixates on hair as disease vector, compares bar to barber shop
jokes.explanation: Frank's paranoia about hair leading to bizarre comparison

jokes.text: That's what carries the disease, Dennis-- the hair. We don't really need it. Look at us. We're evolving without it. We came from the apes, and look at us now. One day, we'll be completely hairless and totally pure.
jokes.setup: Frank is obsessed with hair as disease vector
jokes.punchline: Creates elaborate pseudoscientific theory about hairless human evolution
jokes.explanation: Frank's paranoia evolving into full conspiracy theory about human evolution

jokes.text: Were you eating pizza and drinking beer up there? Yes, Frank, I was eating and drinking in a ceiling. I mean, will you use your head?
jokes.setup: Frank accuses Dee of eating in the ceiling
jokes.punchline: Dee sarcastically responds pointing out how absurd that would be
jokes.explanation: Dee's sarcastic response to Frank's paranoid accusation

jokes.text: That's not mine.
jokes.setup: Frank finds evidence of rule-breaking
jokes.punchline: Dee's weak denial of obvious evidence
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective denial in face of obvious evidence

jokes.text: Trust me, Dee, if I found myself getting sick, I would simply say... 'Sickness be gone!'
jokes.setup: Dennis claims he can control his body perfectly
jokes.punchline: Believes he can literally command sickness away with words
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis narcissism - believing he can control illness through sheer will and commands

jokes.text: Uh, movie, movie. Two, three, four, five words. Doesn't matter? Ocean's Eleven. Ocean's Twelve! Ocean's Thirteen!
jokes.setup: They're playing charades to pass time
jokes.punchline: Frank just guesses Ocean's movies regardless of clues
jokes.explanation: Frank's charades strategy of just guessing Ocean's movies regardless of clues

jokes.text: Frank, I can't concentrate when you look like this. You look ridiculous, man. It was What's Eating Gilbert Grape?
jokes.setup: Frank has shaved all his hair off and looks bizarre
jokes.punchline: Charlie can't focus on charades because Frank looks so ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Visual gag of Frank's appearance being so distracting it ruins their game

jokes.text: He breached! He breached! His suit ripped in the store! He tried to do, like a power spin, and it ripped. And he probably got sick!
jokes.setup: Charlie has been hiding Mac's suit breach
jokes.punchline: Finally confesses in panic, revealing absurd cause (power spin)
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Mac's dance move causing the breach, with Charlie's paranoid confession

jokes.text: Does that sound like a man who needs to be in a hospital?
jokes.setup: Dennis just made horrible sickly sounds
jokes.punchline: Asks if that sounds like someone who needs medical help
jokes.explanation: Dennis's denial in face of obvious evidence of severe illness

jokes.text: Charlie, look at that. Look how clean I am. Dude, you look like a shriveled raisin.
jokes.setup: Frank has obsessively cleaned himself
jokes.punchline: Charlie accurately describes his horrifying appearance
jokes.explanation: Visual gag of Frank's over-cleaning making him look worse, not better

jokes.text: When I get out of here, I'm gonna clean Philadelphia. Good. A giant cleanse. Well, good luck with that. Not the city... the population.
jokes.setup: Frank talks about cleaning Philadelphia
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he means killing people, not cleaning buildings
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation from cleaning to genocide, delivered with perfect economy and Frank's casual tone

jokes.text: Please, Mac, I've been vaccinated for every disease in the book. I'm gonna be just fine. Oh, God.
jokes.setup: Charlie boasts about being immune to all diseases
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets sick right after claiming immunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing as Charlie gets sick immediately after boasting immunity

jokes.text: I knew you drank cleaning supplies. Oh, no, dude, this is booze. I stash whiskey in here.
jokes.setup: Mac thinks Charlie is drinking cleaning supplies
jokes.punchline: Charlie reveals he hides whiskey in cleaning supply bottles
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie reveal about his hidden drinking habits and creative storage solutions

jokes.text: Dee stashes warm beer in the ceiling of the back office. I keep a flask of créme de menthe behind the jukebox in the bar, it's not a big deal.
jokes.setup: Charlie explains his hidden alcohol
jokes.punchline: Reveals everyone has elaborate secret drinking stashes throughout the bar
jokes.explanation: Escalates to reveal the extent of their hidden drinking problem throughout the bar

jokes.text: Oh, my God, you guys. You guys, we haven't been drinking since we quarantined ourselves into the bar. At least since we got stuck in the bathroom. Wait, wait, wait, so what are you saying? That-that we never had the flu? We were just having alcohol withdrawal?
jokes.setup: They suddenly feel better after drinking
jokes.punchline: Realize they weren't sick, just going through alcohol withdrawal
jokes.explanation: Major plot revelation that recontextualizes the entire episode - they're all severe alcoholics

jokes.text: Is it great? It means we're all alcoholics. Ah... Yeah, yeah... right. What do we do with that information? What do you do with any information? You just stuff it deep down inside and keep an eye on it.
jokes.setup: They realize they're all alcoholics
jokes.punchline: Dennis's solution is to just ignore the information and repress it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis philosophy about dealing with uncomfortable self-knowledge through repression

jokes.text: I'm certainly not gonna stop drinking. I mean, I physically can't. No, no, not at this point. We're in, we're in too deep.
jokes.setup: They acknowledge their alcoholism
jokes.punchline: Decide they're too far gone to stop drinking
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy as they rationalize continuing their destructive behavior

jokes.text: You've got a key?! We've been in here this whole time, and you could've let us out?! I didn't know that we were trapped in here. I thought we were just playing quarantine.
jokes.setup: They discover Charlie had a key the whole time
jokes.punchline: Charlie didn't realize they were actually trapped
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie obliviousness that recontextualizes the entire episode's premise

jokes.text: Frank? Oh, my God! What the hell did you do to yourself? I just want to be pure.
jokes.setup: They find Frank covered in hand sanitizer
jokes.punchline: Frank's simple explanation for his horrifying appearance
jokes.explanation: Frank's obsession with purity taken to horrifying extreme, delivered with perfect economy

jokes.text: Boyz II Men? That concert is canceled. What? Why? Yeah, yeah, they all got the flu. Oh, son of a bitch! So we did this for nothing?
jokes.setup: They arrive at the Boyz II Men audition
jokes.punchline: The concert is canceled because Boyz II Men got the flu they were avoiding
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic ending where the thing they were protecting themselves from got their idols instead

jokes.text: I mean, guys, look, we came together in harmony. You know, if I'm being honest, I'd rather go back to the bar and get drunk, than go to some, you know, concert.
jokes.setup: Dennis tries to find meaning in their failed quarantine
jokes.punchline: Admits he'd rather drink than perform anyway
jokes.explanation: Dennis trying to spin their failure into something positive while revealing his true priorities

jokes.text: You ready for your sponge bath? Shit.
jokes.setup: Frank is covered in hand sanitizer and needs to be cleaned
jokes.punchline: Charlie offers a sponge bath, Frank realizes his predicament
jokes.explanation: Awkward ending with Frank realizing he needs intimate help from Charlie
